_comment: Compat matrix per #1027. This file is the spec-driven inventory of every prop / style / API surface Pulp's React + DOM-lite consumers might use, mapped to its current implementation status. Status values: supported | partial | missing | wontfix. The 'mapsTo' field names the bridge / Yoga / Skia path the prop lowers to. The 'tests' field is intentionally empty here and will be filled in by individual fix PRs that ship Catch2 / vitest coverage. The audit was generated 2026-04-30 by walking three reference specs (Yoga via React Native Layout Props, React Native View / Text / Transform Style Props, and the most-used MDN CSS reference) and cross-referencing against four implementation surfaces: packages/pulp-react/src/prop-applier.ts (the @pulp/react switch), core/view/js/web-compat-style-decl.js (the DOM-lite CSS adapter), core/view/src/widget_bridge.cpp (the C++ bridge — 251 register_function entries), and core/view/include/pulp/view/{view,geometry}.hpp (the Flex / View widget surface).

spec_sources.yoga: https://www.yogalayout.dev/docs/styling — list cross-checked against https://reactnative.dev/docs/layout-props which mirrors the upstream Yoga API
spec_sources.css: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/Reference/Properties — top ~150 commonly-used properties across layout, box-model, typography, color, transforms, flex/grid, opacity. Print, paged-media, and CSS-fonts loading specifics are out of scope.
spec_sources.html_element: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element + HTMLElement attribute coverage, ARIA attributes, dataset, classList, the DocumentFragment shim

_audit._notes_on_consumer_surfaces: The same prop can have different status across consumer surfaces. 'css/*' tracks what core/view/js/web-compat-style-decl.js (the DOM-lite el.style adapter) routes; 'rn/*' tracks what packages/pulp-react/src/prop-applier.ts (the React renderer for @pulp/react JSX intrinsics) routes; 'yoga/*' tracks the underlying Yoga-shaped layout surface in core/view/include/pulp/view/geometry.hpp::FlexStyle; 'canvas2d/*' tracks the Canvas2D shim in web-compat-canvas.js paired with the canvas* family in widget_bridge.cpp + the Skia/CG backends in core/canvas/; 'html/*' tracks the DOM-lite Element / HTMLElement surface in web-compat-element.js + web-compat-document.js. Where a prop is supported by one consumer but not another, both are noted explicitly in 'notes'.

_changelog.2026-04-30: Initial population — 361 entries across css/, rn/, yoga/. react/, html/, canvas2d/ stubbed. PR #1166.
_changelog.2026-05-04: Refresh after PRs #1167, #1169, #1173, #1174, #1291, #1297, #1344, #1345, #1347, #1348, #1353. Populated canvas2d/ (~60 entries) and html/ (~30 entries). Added rn/d, rn/viewBox, rn/fill, rn/stroke, rn/strokeWidth, rn/overlay (SvgPath + overlay-routing). Updated css/border, css/borderRadius, css/borderColor, css/fontFamily status to reflect per-attribute setter routing. Added css/_hover_pseudo for :hover stylesheet support.
_changelog.2026-05-05: pulp #1434 batch 4: React Native shorthand aliases marginHorizontal / marginVertical / paddingHorizontal / paddingVertical now fan out at the JS-side translators (web-compat-style-decl.js + prop-applier.ts) to the existing per-edge bridge setters. Reclassified rn/* + yoga/* entries from missing -> supported and added new css/* entries (4 new keys; css count 195 -> 199).

css/__hover_pseudo.mapsTo: web-compat-document.js (#1345 / pulp #1149 part b): `:hover` rules are parsed out of the inline <style> element, stashed per-element, and the document installs mouseenter/mouseleave listeners that swap the styles on the fly.
css/__hover_pseudo.notes: Pseudo-class support is intentionally narrow. `:hover` is the highest-impact CSS selector in interactive UIs and the most-requested by Spectr/FilterBank consumers. The other interactive pseudo-classes (`:focus`, `:active`) require additional bridge plumbing and are not yet wired.

css/__pseudo_classes_note.notes: Pulp routes hover/click/etc. through bridge event registrations (registerHover, registerClick) rather than through CSS pseudo-classes. JSX consumers wire interactivity via on* event props, not via CSS.

css/alignContent.mapsTo: web-compat calls setFlex(id, 'align_content', ...) but setFlex switch has NO 'align_content' branch
css/alignContent.notes: MAJOR GAP -- silently dropped. Multi-line flex layouts have no cross-axis alignment control.

css/alignItems.notes: FlexAlign enum has {start, center, end, stretch, auto_} -- no baseline support.

css/animation.mapsTo: parseTransition + setAnimation -- but setAnimation is a TODO no-op
css/animation.notes: MAJOR GAP -- widget_bridge.cpp:3208 setAnimation is registered but the body is `(void)id; (void)prop;...` -- a stub. CSS @keyframes animations are silently dropped.

css/aspectRatio.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js parses '<n>', '<num>/<den>', and 'auto'; calls setFlex(id, 'aspect_ratio', ratio); widget_bridge writes FlexStyle.aspect_ratio; yoga_layout dispatches to YGNodeStyleSetAspectRatio
css/aspectRatio.notes: pulp #1434 — three value forms accepted: plain number ('1.5'), 'width / height' ratio ('16/9' -> 1.778), and 'auto' (clears slot). Both 'aspectRatio' (camelCase) and 'aspect-ratio' (kebab-case) work; setProperty auto-converts kebab to camel.

css/backdropFilter.notes: Blur-only. CSS shim parses the blur(Npx) substring; non-matching filter functions are ignored. None / empty clears the slot.

css/background.mapsTo: if 'gradient' in value -> setBackgroundGradient; else parseCSSColor -> setBackground
css/background.notes: Background shorthand supports only color OR a gradient.

css/backgroundColor.notes: Modern color spaces not supported by parseCSSColor.

css/backgroundPosition.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundPosition if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/backgroundRepeat.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundRepeat if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/backgroundSize.mapsTo: web-compat calls setBackgroundSize if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/backgroundSize.notes: Silent no-op. Tied to backgroundImage url() which is also missing.

css/border.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'border' shorthand parses '<width>px solid <color>' and routes to setBorderColor(id, color) + setBorderWidth(id, width) (per-attribute, NOT the unified setBorder, to preserve any prior borderRadius). 'borderColor' / 'borderWidth' / 'borderRadius' route to their respective per-attribute setters too — see pulp #1166/#1169.
css/border.notes: Fixed 2026-04-26 (#1169 / audit PR #1166 finding #4): per-attribute setters now preserve unset siblings, matching CSS semantics. Setting `borderColor` after `borderRadius` no longer zeros the radius. The unified setBorder(id, color, width, radius) is still available for the React `border` object-prop path. Border style ('solid', 'dashed', 'dotted', 'double') is parsed but only the default solid pen is rendered today.

css/borderColor.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'borderColor' -> setBorderColor(id, color) — per-attribute, preserves width and radius.
css/borderColor.notes: Fixed 2026-04-26 (#1169). Previously routed onto the unified setBorder which clobbered radius. Now uses the dedicated setBorderColor bridge fn.

css/borderRadius.notes: Fixed 2026-04-26 (#1169). Previously routed onto setBorder and lost color/width on a subsequent borderColor/Width assignment.

css/borderTopColor.notes: Sets width to 0 -- must combine with borderTopWidth. Quirk.

css/bottom.notes: Same limitation as top.  pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values now flow through to the bridge via the 'NN%' string path; the bridge routes percent values to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

css/boxShadow.mapsTo: regex parse -> setBoxShadow(id, dx, dy, blur, spread, color, inset)
css/boxShadow.notes: Single-shadow only. Multiple comma-separated shadows are not split. pulp #1434 Triage #15: catalog status flipped supported→partial — single-shadow with optional inset is wired (web-compat-style-decl.js:565); multi-shadow comma-separated lists are deferred.

css/boxSizing.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js calls setBoxSizing if defined -- but the bridge does NOT register setBoxSizing
css/boxSizing.notes: Silent no-op. typeof guard in JS prevents a runtime error but the value is never honored. Yoga's box model is content-box-equivalent.

css/columns.notes: Multi-column layout out of scope; not aligned with Yoga or RN.

css/direction.notes: RTL writing direction not modeled. Affects logical inset/margin/padding mapping (currently always LTR).

css/display.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'display' -> setVisible(id, false) on 'none', setVisible(id, true) on 'flex'/'block'. 'grid' is acknowledged but only takes effect when gridTemplateColumns/Rows is also set. Yoga has no 'block', 'inline', 'inline-block', 'table', 'contents' modes -- Pulp is flex-only.
css/display.notes: Yoga's underlying view model is exclusively flex. 'block' is silently aliased to 'flex'. RN's display enum is {none, flex, contents} which matches us better than CSS. The Display enum on the C++ View has no direct field -- visibility is implemented via View::set_visible. There is no 'partial' display rendering -- non-flex values render as flex with default direction. 2026-04-28 (#1167 / pulp #1147): `display: flex` now defaults flex-direction to 'row' to match CSS web compat (Pulp's underlying widgets default to 'col' / FlexDirection::column from RN convention, so the JS shim emits an explicit 'row' when the consumer didn't declare flexDirection / flex-direction / flex-flow with a direction token).

css/filter.notes: Bridge parses 'blur(Npx)'. Other filter functions pass through verbatim.

css/flex.notes: The CSS shorthand keywords (auto/none/initial) parse as NaN and silently set flex_grow=0.

css/flexBasis.notes: Yoga supports 'auto' and percentages; Pulp accepts only px float. pulp #1434 rn batch C: % and auto wired via FlexStyle::dim_flex_basis.

css/flexDirection.notes: MAJOR GAP -- Pulp's FlexDirection enum (geometry.hpp:61) only has {row, column}. Reverse modes silently fall through to default 'col'.

css/flexWrap.notes: FlexStyle.flex_wrap is a bool -- wrap-reverse cannot be expressed.

css/float.notes: CSS float not relevant under a flex-only layout model.

css/fontFamily.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'fontFamily' -> splits on commas, trims, strips matching outer quotes, picks the first non-empty token, calls setFontFamily(id, name). PR #1174.
css/fontFamily.notes: Fixed 2026-04-29 (#1174 / pulp #1151). Generic fallbacks ('monospace', 'ui-monospace', 'sans-serif') are not specially recognized — Skia falls back to the platform default if SkFontMgr can't resolve any of the named families.

css/fontVariant.notes: RN supports fontVariant; Pulp does not.

css/fontWeight.notes: Keyword-to-numeric: normal→400, bold→700, lighter→300, bolder→700. Numeric keywords parse unchanged. Mirrored in @pulp/react prop-applier (_normalizeFontWeight).

css/gap.notes: Two-value 'gap: 10px 20px' is not handled by parseCSSLength -- it parses only the first token.

css/gridTemplateColumns.notes: Forwarded as a string. Renderer honors only what the underlying GridStyle parser accepts. CSS Grid is largely unsupported because Yoga is flex-only.

css/height.notes: Same as width. dim_height supports DimensionUnit::vw/vh/percent/vmin/vmax internally but the setFlex bridge only accepts a single double.

css/inset.notes: Shorthand expands correctly. Underlying setTop/etc. are px only.

css/justifyContent.notes: FlexJustify enum covers the 6 RN values.

css/left.notes: Same limitation as top.  pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values now flow through to the bridge via the 'NN%' string path; the bridge routes percent values to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

css/lineClamp.mapsTo: web-compat calls setLineClamp if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/lineClamp.notes: Silent no-op. Both 'line-clamp' and '-webkit-line-clamp' route here.

css/lineHeight.notes: MAJOR GAP -- unitless line-height is the most common form; we drop it.

css/listStyle.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. list-style shorthand for <ul>/<ol>. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/listStyleImage.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. custom bullet image for <ul>/<ol>. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/listStylePosition.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. inside/outside bullet placement. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/listStyleType.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. 'none' / 'disc' / 'decimal' — `list-style-type: none` is the most common reset in design-tool HTML exports. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/margin.notes: 'margin: auto' centers in CSS -- Pulp does NOT honor auto margins.

css/marginBlock.notes: Logical property; assumes horizontal writing mode.

css/marginHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: React Native shorthand alias surfaced through the DOM-lite el.style adapter so RN snippets ported verbatim work in the CSS surface too. Web CSS does not define this property; provided for cross-tool import-readiness.

css/marginInline.notes: Logical property normalized to physical. No RTL awareness -- always treated as LTR (margin_left = inline-start).

css/marginInlineStart.mapsTo: case in switch is bare break (line 609); RTL untracked
css/marginInlineStart.notes: pulp #1434 batch 1: status flipped missing → partial. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:784 maps to setFlex(margin_left) under LTR assumption; RTL not yet wired.

css/marginVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: React Native shorthand alias surfaced through the DOM-lite el.style adapter so RN snippets ported verbatim work in the CSS surface too. Web CSS does not define this property; provided for cross-tool import-readiness.

css/maxHeight.notes: Same as max-width. pulp #1434 rn batch C: % values now route through FlexStyle::dim_*.unit → Yoga percent API.

css/maxWidth.notes: 0 = no maximum (semantic differs from CSS where unspecified = none). pulp #1434 rn batch C: % values now route through FlexStyle::dim_*.unit → Yoga percent API.

css/minHeight.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: % values now route through FlexStyle::dim_*.unit → Yoga percent API.

css/minWidth.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: % values now route through FlexStyle::dim_*.unit → Yoga percent API.

css/mixBlendMode.notes: RN supports mixBlendMode in New Architecture; Pulp does not.

css/outline.mapsTo: web-compat calls setOutline if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/outline.notes: pulp #1434 batch 1: status flipped missing → partial. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:525 routes through setOutline. Single-keyword + length + color forms are wired.

css/outlineColor.notes: pulp #1434 batch 1: status flipped missing → partial. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:538 routes through setOutline (color slot).

css/outlineWidth.notes: pulp #1434 batch 1: status flipped missing → partial. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:533 routes through setOutline (width slot). Numeric px values supported.

css/overflow.notes: View::Overflow enum has only {visible, hidden}. Scroll requires using the ScrollView intrinsic.

css/paddingHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: React Native shorthand alias surfaced through the DOM-lite el.style adapter so RN snippets ported verbatim work in the CSS surface too. Web CSS does not define this property; provided for cross-tool import-readiness.

css/paddingVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: React Native shorthand alias surfaced through the DOM-lite el.style adapter so RN snippets ported verbatim work in the CSS surface too. Web CSS does not define this property; provided for cross-tool import-readiness.

css/placeContent.notes: align_content half is silently dropped (see css/alignContent).

css/placeItems.notes: Misnamed -- second token is mapped to justify_content rather than justify_items (which Yoga doesn't support).

css/position.mapsTo: web-compat-style-decl.js: 'position' -> setPosition. View::Position enum has static_/relative/absolute/fixed/sticky.
css/position.notes: All five CSS values are accepted by the bridge. 'fixed' and 'sticky' may render the same as 'absolute' in practice -- see #779 follow-ups for behavioral verification.

css/printMargin.notes: Print media is out of scope for a GPU plugin renderer.

css/right.notes: Same limitation as top.  pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values now flow through to the bridge via the 'NN%' string path; the bridge routes percent values to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

css/scrollBehavior.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. 'smooth' scroll on link / programmatic scroll. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/scrollMargin.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. scroll-snap landing area. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/scrollPadding.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. scroll-snap landing area. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/scrollSnapType.notes: pulp #1434 batch 2: reclassified OOS → NOT-IMPL. scroll-snap container; mobile carousel / hero gallery primitive. Common in design-tool exports (Figma / v0 / Claude Design HTML); track for impl follow-up.

css/textDecoration.notes: Single-keyword only. Cannot express 'underline dashed red'.

css/textDecorationColor.notes: Bridge calls Label::set_text_decoration_color. Painted color falls back to the text color when unset.

css/textDecorationLine.notes: Routes to the same Label::TextDecoration setter as the shorthand. Per-attribute longhand preserves any previously-set sibling longhand (color/style). The deprecated 'blink' value is intentionally not supported (matches modern UA behavior).

css/textDecorationStyle.notes: CSS shim + bridge accept all 5 spec values and store them on Label. Paint path renders solid regardless today; non-solid styles fall back to solid (spec-allowed).

css/textShadow.mapsTo: web-compat calls setTextShadow if defined -- bridge does NOT register it
css/textShadow.notes: pulp #1434 batch 1: status flipped missing → partial. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:557 parses `Xpx Ypx Bpx <color>` and calls setTextShadow. Multi-shadow lists are not yet split.

css/top.notes: parseCSSLength is invoked but the bridge stores raw px only -- percentage values are dropped. setTop accepts numbers only.  pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values now flow through to the bridge via the 'NN%' string path; the bridge routes percent values to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

css/touchAction.mapsTo: stored on element instance; not propagated to bridge
css/touchAction.notes: Set but not consumed by the C++ hit-test path.

css/transform.mapsTo: parseTransform -> setScale | setRotation | setTranslate (and setTransform matrix); transformX/Y separately
css/transform.notes: MAJOR GAP -- only 5 of CSS transform's ~12 functions are honored. RN supports a richer set.

css/transformOrigin.mapsTo: parse keywords + percentages -> setTransformOrigin(id, x, y) (0..1)
css/transformOrigin.notes: Px values are mishandled -- only % and keywords resolve correctly.

css/transition.notes: Pulp ignores the property name and the timing function; only duration is propagated. 2026-05-01 (#1345 / pulp #1149 part b): the document stylesheet now supports `:hover` selectors via mouseenter/mouseleave bridge listeners. CSS rules under `:hover` are stashed and re-applied per-element on hover-in, restored on hover-out — completing the transition story end-to-end for the common interactive case.

css/transitionProperty.notes: All declared transitions animate everything (or nothing depending on backend). Cannot scope.

css/verticalAlign.notes: Not relevant in flex-only layout. Could add for Label baseline alignment.

css/visibility.mapsTo: setVisibility(id, 'visible'|'hidden') if registered, else opacity fallback
css/visibility.notes: setVisibility IS registered (widget_bridge.cpp:1814) so the primary path works.

css/whiteSpace.notes: Bridge accepts the string but layout enforcement varies.

css/width.notes: FlexStyle.preferred_width is a float. Yoga in upstream supports % and auto, but Pulp's C++ port loses that on the JSON wire -- only floats are accepted by setFlex.

css/willChange.notes: Hint to compositor -- Pulp's renderer is GPU all the time.

css/wordBreak.mapsTo: web-compat calls setWordBreak if defined -- bridge does NOT register it

css/wordWrap.notes: Legacy alias of overflow-wrap. Silent no-op.

rn/alignContent.mapsTo: @pulp/react does not expose alignContent on FlexProps
rn/alignContent.notes: Symmetric with css/alignContent -- also missing in the @pulp/react TS surface (types.ts FlexProps).

rn/alignItems.notes: Pulp's FlexAlign type takes short forms only ({start, center, end, stretch}). RN documentation uses 'flex-start'/'flex-end' which prop-applier passes through verbatim -- these would not match the C++ enum names. Style decl converts 'flex-start'->'start' but @pulp/react does not.

rn/aspectRatio.notes: pulp #1434 — JSX surface accepts numeric aspectRatio (RN parity per oracle 'kind: number'). String forms ('16/9', 'auto') reach FlexStyle through the CSS shim path (web-compat-style-decl.js), not the @pulp/react TS surface.

rn/backfaceVisibility.mapsTo: Bridge has setBackfaceVisibility -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/backgroundColor.notes: TS type for `background` is `string` (hex-only by convention).

rn/borderBottomColor.notes: pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported, claim the wired surface (#1396 was the wiring PR).

rn/borderBottomLeftRadius.notes: Same. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderBottomRightRadius.notes: Same. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderBottomWidth.notes: RN prop is flat top-level, Pulp uses object shape. Convert: <View style={{borderBottomWidth: 2}} /> -> <View borderBottom={{color:'#000', width:2}} />. Awkward for RN ports. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderColor.notes: pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderLeftColor.notes: pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported, claim the wired surface (#1396 was the wiring PR).

rn/borderLeftWidth.notes: Same as borderBottomWidth. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderRadius.notes: Workaround: <View border={{color:'transparent', width:0, radius:R}} />. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderRightColor.notes: pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported, claim the wired surface (#1396 was the wiring PR).

rn/borderRightWidth.notes: Same. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderTopColor.notes: pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported, claim the wired surface (#1396 was the wiring PR).

rn/borderTopLeftRadius.notes: Bridge has setBorderTopLeftRadius -- needs prop wiring. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderTopRightRadius.notes: Same. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderTopWidth.notes: Same as borderBottomWidth. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/borderWidth.notes: User can call <View border={{color:'#000', width:N}} /> as workaround. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported.

rn/bottom.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent strings flow through prop-applier verbatim; the bridge routes them to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

rn/boxShadow.mapsTo: @pulp/react prop-applier dispatches `boxShadow` (string OR object form) to setBoxShadow / clearBoxShadow (prop-applier.ts:323+). String parser mirrors web-compat-style-decl.js.
supportedValues: object form { offsetX, offsetY, blur?, spread?, color, inset? }
rn/boxShadow.notes: Could be exposed analogously to `border={...}` shape. pulp #1434 Triage #15: surfaced at the @pulp/react TS layer. Both string and object forms route to setBoxShadow; multi-shadow remains a deferred gap (single-shadow path covers the bulk of Figma / Tailwind / v0 emissions).

rn/boxSizing.mapsTo: no branch (and bridge does not register setBoxSizing)

rn/cursor.mapsTo: Bridge has setCursor -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/d.mapsTo: prop-applier.ts: 'd' -> setSvgPath(id, value). Wired by SvgPath JSX intrinsic (pulp #994 / PR #1291).
supportedValues: any SVG path-data string (e.g. 'M0,0 L10,10 Z')
rn/d.notes: Only meaningful when the consumer renders <SvgPath> from @pulp/react. The bridge createSvgPath / setSvgPath / setSvgViewBox / setSvgFill / setSvgStroke / setSvgStrokeWidth surface lands on SvgPathWidget in core/view/.

rn/display.mapsTo: @pulp/react prop-applier dispatches `display: 'flex'` to setVisible(true) and `display: 'none'` to setVisible(false) (pulp #1434 Triage #12, prop-applier.ts:323+).
rn/display.notes: @pulp/react users opt out via the boolean `visible` prop. No 'contents' equivalent. pulp #1434 (Triage #12): the prop-applier now accepts display: flex / none, mirroring the yoga side wired in #1422. "contents" remains unsupported (Yoga has no equivalent).

rn/elevation.notes: Android-only Material elevation. Pulp uses boxShadow as the cross-platform equivalent.

rn/experimental_backgroundImage.mapsTo: @pulp/react has backgroundGradient; CSS gradient strings only.

rn/fill.mapsTo: prop-applier.ts: 'fill' -> setSvgFill(id, value). Bridge accepts a CSS-color string.
unsupportedValues: url(#gradient) — gradient fills not yet wired through SvgPathWidget
rn/fill.notes: Only on @pulp/react SvgPath. Inline <svg> in DOM-lite still uses createCol (pulp #1147), which means CSS `fill` does NOT route to SVG fill — it falls into the unknown-prop default switch.

rn/filter.mapsTo: Bridge has setFilter -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/flex.mapsTo: no shorthand prop in @pulp/react. Components must use flexGrow/flexShrink/flexBasis individually.
rn/flex.notes: Common RN pattern; awkward to port.

rn/flexBasis.notes: TS type is 'number' only. pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge accepts number / "NN%" / "auto"; FlexStyle::dim_flex_basis.unit drives YGNodeStyleSetFlexBasis{,Percent,Auto}.

rn/flexDirection.mapsTo: prop-applier 'direction' -> setFlex(id, 'direction', value); but RN says 'row'|'row-reverse'|'column'|'column-reverse'
rn/flexDirection.notes: ALSO MAJOR -- types.ts FlexDirection = 'row' | 'col'. Pulp uses 'col' instead of 'column' (RN convention) -- porting RN code requires renaming. And no reverse modes.

rn/flexWrap.notes: TS type is `boolean` -- RN's enum has 'wrap-reverse' which we cannot express.

rn/fontFamily.mapsTo: intentionally NOT dispatched in prop-applier.ts (line 152 comment) -- pending #932 SkFontMgr fix
rn/fontFamily.notes: Documented gap in prop-applier; reflects pulp#932 / pulp#927.

rn/fontWeight.notes: Keyword aliasing not done in prop-applier.

rn/height.notes: TS type is `number` only. pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_height.unit. auto remains a follow-up.

rn/inset.mapsTo: no @pulp/react prop. CSS shim has it.

rn/justifyContent.notes: Long-form RN names not auto-converted -- same trap as alignItems.

rn/left.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent strings flow through prop-applier verbatim; the bridge routes them to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

rn/marginHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand fans out to the per-edge bridge setters at the @pulp/react entry point.

rn/marginVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand fans out to the per-edge bridge setters at the @pulp/react entry point.

rn/maxHeight.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_max_height.unit; yoga_layout.cpp routes to YGNodeStyleSetMaxHeightPercent. pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_max_height.unit → YGNodeStyleSetMaxHeightPercent.

rn/maxWidth.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_max_width.unit; yoga_layout.cpp routes to YGNodeStyleSetMaxWidthPercent. pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_max_width.unit → YGNodeStyleSetMaxWidthPercent.

rn/minHeight.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_min_height.unit; yoga_layout.cpp routes to YGNodeStyleSetMinHeightPercent. pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_min_height.unit → YGNodeStyleSetMinHeightPercent.

rn/minWidth.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_min_width.unit; yoga_layout.cpp routes to YGNodeStyleSetMinWidthPercent. pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_min_width.unit → YGNodeStyleSetMinWidthPercent.

rn/overflow.notes: Plumbing missing in prop-applier; ScrollView intrinsic exposes scroll. pulp #1434: catalog hygiene — flipped missing → supported. View::Overflow has only {visible, hidden}; scroll is a separate ScrollView container concern.

rn/overlay.mapsTo: prop-applier.ts: 'overlay' -> claimOverlay(id) / releaseOverlay(id) on flip. Bridge serializes onto a single View::active_overlay_ slot to fix React-popover click routing (pulp #1148, PRs #1297 + #1344).
rn/overlay.notes: Generalized overlay-click routing. The CSS-shim auto-claim heuristic (web-compat-style-decl.js _reevaluateOverlay) calls into the same bridge functions when position:absolute combines with z-index above a threshold (>= 10) OR the data-overlay='true' hint is set, so DOM-lite consumers don't need to rewire to the @pulp/react overlay prop.

rn/paddingHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand fans out to the per-edge bridge setters at the @pulp/react entry point.

rn/paddingVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand fans out to the per-edge bridge setters at the @pulp/react entry point.

rn/pointerEvents.mapsTo: Bridge has setPointerEvents -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/position.notes: RN's enum is {absolute, relative, static}. Pulp accepts more (fixed, sticky) than RN.

rn/right.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent strings flow through prop-applier verbatim; the bridge routes them to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

rn/shadowColor.mapsTo: no branch -- would route to setShadow / setBoxShadow
rn/shadowColor.notes: iOS shadow* family -- superseded by boxShadow in modern RN.

rn/stroke.mapsTo: prop-applier.ts: 'stroke' -> setSvgStroke(id, value). Bridge accepts a CSS-color string.
rn/stroke.notes: Only on @pulp/react SvgPath. See rn/fill caveat — no DOM-lite path-prop equivalent.

rn/textAlignVertical.notes: Android-only in RN; could map to flex align.

rn/textDecorationLine.mapsTo: no branch (use textDecoration in CSS surface only)
rn/textDecorationLine.notes: Use bridge setTextDecoration via CSS surface; @pulp/react does not surface.

rn/textTransform.mapsTo: Bridge has setTextTransform -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/top.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent strings flow through prop-applier verbatim; the bridge routes them to YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent via View::top_unit_/etc.

rn/transform.mapsTo: no @pulp/react prop. CSS surface routes through setScale/setRotation/setTranslate but with reduced function set.
rn/transform.notes: MAJOR -- RN's transform is an array of objects; @pulp/react has none of it.

rn/transformOrigin.mapsTo: Bridge has setTransformOrigin -- not surfaced in @pulp/react TS.

rn/viewBox.mapsTo: prop-applier.ts: 'viewBox' -> setSvgViewBox(id, w, h). Expected as [number, number] tuple.
unsupportedValues: SVG-style 'min-x min-y w h' string (only the [w,h] tuple form is wired)
rn/viewBox.notes: RN doesn't have a native SVG surface; this is a Pulp-specific @pulp/react intrinsic. CSS-shim consumers don't see this prop today.

rn/width.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: prop-applier forwards string|number; bridge dispatches percent via FlexStyle::dim_width.unit (#1426 path). auto remains a follow-up.

yoga/flexDirection.notes: FlexDirection enum at geometry.hpp:61 has only 2 values. Yoga upstream has 4.

yoga/flexWrap.notes: Bool can only express two states.

yoga/flexBasis.notes: Yoga upstream supports YGAuto / percent; Pulp accepts only px float. pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge accepts NN% and the "auto" keyword; FlexStyle::dim_flex_basis drives YGNodeStyleSetFlexBasis{,Percent,Auto}. content remains unsupported.

yoga/justifyContent.notes: All 6 modes covered. Bridge accepts both CSS spelling (flex-start/flex-end) and Yoga spelling (start/end) — CSS translator _cssToFlex maps the former to the latter.

yoga/alignItems.notes: Yoga has YGAlignBaseline; Pulp does not.

yoga/alignContent.mapsTo: no field on FlexStyle and no setFlex case for 'align_content'
yoga/alignContent.notes: MAJOR -- multi-line flex cross-axis distribution unimplementable.

yoga/width.notes: pulp #1423: percent values route through FlexStyle.dim_width (DimensionUnit::percent) and yoga_layout.cpp calls YGNodeStyleSetWidthPercent. CSS translator passes 'NN%' strings verbatim to the bridge. `auto` remains unsupported (deferred follow-up).

yoga/height.notes: pulp #1423: same as width (YGNodeStyleSetHeightPercent path via FlexStyle.dim_height). `auto` remains unsupported (deferred follow-up).

yoga/minWidth.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge populates FlexStyle::dim_min_width.unit; yoga_layout.cpp dispatches to YGNodeStyleSetMinWidthPercent.

yoga/minHeight.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge populates FlexStyle::dim_min_height.unit; yoga_layout.cpp dispatches to YGNodeStyleSetMinHeightPercent.

yoga/maxWidth.notes: Semantic of 0 differs from CSS unspecified. pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge populates FlexStyle::dim_max_width.unit; yoga_layout.cpp dispatches to YGNodeStyleSetMaxWidthPercent.

yoga/maxHeight.notes: pulp #1434 rn batch C: bridge populates FlexStyle::dim_max_height.unit; yoga_layout.cpp dispatches to YGNodeStyleSetMaxHeightPercent.

yoga/aspectRatio.notes: pulp #1434 — added FlexStyle.aspect_ratio + Yoga dispatch + bridge setFlex 'aspect_ratio' branch + @pulp/react aspectRatio prop. CSS shim accepts plain numbers ('1.5'), 'width/height' ratios ('16/9'), and 'auto' (clears slot).

yoga/margin.notes: Yoga has YGAuto for margin centering; not supported.

yoga/marginHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand. No dedicated FlexStyle field; the JS-side translators (DOM-lite + @pulp/react) decompose to the existing per-edge slots before reaching the bridge. Inherits the per-edge yoga gap on auto (same as yoga/marginLeft).

yoga/marginVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand. No dedicated FlexStyle field; the JS-side translators (DOM-lite + @pulp/react) decompose to the existing per-edge slots before reaching the bridge. Inherits the per-edge yoga gap on auto (same as yoga/marginTop).

yoga/paddingHorizontal.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand. No dedicated FlexStyle field; the JS-side translators (DOM-lite + @pulp/react) decompose to the existing per-edge slots before reaching the bridge. Inherits the per-edge yoga gap on % (same as yoga/paddingLeft).

yoga/paddingVertical.notes: pulp #1434 batch 4: RN shorthand. No dedicated FlexStyle field; the JS-side translators (DOM-lite + @pulp/react) decompose to the existing per-edge slots before reaching the bridge. Inherits the per-edge yoga gap on % (same as yoga/paddingTop).

yoga/borderWidth.mapsTo: no FlexStyle field -- Pulp handles border purely as a paint overlay (View::set_border_*).
yoga/borderWidth.notes: Yoga's BORDER edges contribute to content-box sizing. Pulp's borders are paint-only, not measured by layout. This breaks parity with Yoga measurement when borders are large.

yoga/position.notes: Pulp's enum is a SUPERSET of Yoga's {static, relative, absolute}.

yoga/top.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values route through View::set_top(v, DimensionUnit::percent); yoga_layout.cpp dispatches on top_unit_/right_unit_/bottom_unit_/left_unit_ to call YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent. CSS translator passes 'NN%' strings verbatim to the bridge. Mirrors the FlexStyle::dim_width path from pulp #1423 (PR #1426) for the View positional fields.

yoga/right.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values route through View::set_right(v, DimensionUnit::percent); yoga_layout.cpp dispatches on top_unit_/right_unit_/bottom_unit_/left_unit_ to call YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent. CSS translator passes 'NN%' strings verbatim to the bridge. Mirrors the FlexStyle::dim_width path from pulp #1423 (PR #1426) for the View positional fields.

yoga/bottom.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values route through View::set_bottom(v, DimensionUnit::percent); yoga_layout.cpp dispatches on top_unit_/right_unit_/bottom_unit_/left_unit_ to call YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent. CSS translator passes 'NN%' strings verbatim to the bridge. Mirrors the FlexStyle::dim_width path from pulp #1423 (PR #1426) for the View positional fields.

yoga/left.notes: pulp #1434 batch 6: percent values route through View::set_left(v, DimensionUnit::percent); yoga_layout.cpp dispatches on top_unit_/right_unit_/bottom_unit_/left_unit_ to call YGNodeStyleSetPositionPercent. CSS translator passes 'NN%' strings verbatim to the bridge. Mirrors the FlexStyle::dim_width path from pulp #1423 (PR #1426) for the View positional fields.

yoga/display.notes: pulp #1420: claim flex+none as supported. CSS translator at web-compat-style-decl.js:86-118 routes display:none to setVisible(false) and display:flex to setVisible(true)+setFlex(direction). 'flex' is implicit in pulp's layout model; 'none' uses View visibility. Yoga has YGDisplay {flex, none, contents}; pulp doesn't model 'contents'.

yoga/overflow.notes: Yoga has YGOverflow {visible, hidden, scroll}; Pulp omits scroll (use ScrollView intrinsic).

yoga/direction.notes: Yoga supports direction for RTL; Pulp does not.

react._note: The @pulp/react reconciler surface (host-config.ts, prop-applier.ts) is captured under rn/* above (prop-applier covers ~70 props). This section is reserved for React-specific features that aren't single-prop entries: Suspense, ConcurrentMode, Profiler, error boundaries, refs, portals, hooks edge cases (useTransition, useDeferredValue, useId, useSyncExternalStore). As of 2026-05-04 the renderer is built on react-reconciler@0.31 and supports the basic function-component + useState + useEffect + useRef + useMemo / useCallback set; concurrent features are not exercised. File-issue follow-up: enumerate observed React features end-to-end and populate per-feature entries. Currently empty pending that audit.

html._note: Populated 2026-05-04 from web-compat-element.js (~836 LOC) and web-compat-document.js (~1527 LOC). Tracks the DOM-lite consumer surface: createElement / setAttribute / appendChild / addEventListener / Element / HTMLElement attributes / dataset / classList / events / style sheet support. Spec source: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Element + HTMLElement subclasses. Recent additions since 2026-04-30: html/svg width/height attribute replay (#1347 / pulp #1147), html/span/p/label widget mapping confirmed (Label widget, not View), html/style :hover stylesheet support (#1345 / pulp #1149 part b).

html/ARIA.mapsTo: ARIA attributes (aria-label, role, etc.) are stored in _attributes but NOT routed to platform accessibility APIs.
html/ARIA.notes: MAJOR GAP — accessibility surface needs dedicated work. File a follow-up issue.

html/Element_addEventListener.mapsTo: Element.addEventListener — registers click/mouseenter/mouseleave/pointerenter/pointerleave/pointerdown/move/up/cancel/gesturestart/change/focus/blur with the bridge. registerHover/registerClick/registerPointer/registerGesture are called as needed.
html/Element_addEventListener.notes: Hover events fixed in PR #1173 (pulp #1149 part a) — registerHover is now called on hover-event registration so the C++ side actually fires. Status flipped to partial (pulp #1434 batch 1) — drag/wheel/keyboard event groups route through different surfaces.

html/Element_appendChild.mapsTo: web-compat-dom-ops.js: appendChild reparents native widget if needed via _reparentNative + replays presentational attrs.

html/Element_classList.mapsTo: Element.classList — add/remove/toggle/contains/length/item, mirrors className. Triggers stylesheet re-application on change.

html/Element_cloneNode.mapsTo: Element.cloneNode(deep) — replicates className, textContent, type, value, style props; recursive when deep.

html/Element_dataset.mapsTo: Element.dataset — kebab-cased data-* attrs surface as camelCase JS properties. Includes the data-overlay='true' hint (#1148).

html/Element_disabled.mapsTo: Element.disabled -> stylesheet re-application; bridge does not currently route to setEnabled.
html/Element_disabled.notes: Re-apply only — does NOT call setEnabled. File a follow-up if needed.

html/Element_dispatchEvent.mapsTo: Element.dispatchEvent — capture + target + bubble phases; honors stopPropagation / preventDefault / _noBubble.

html/Element_nodeName.mapsTo: Returns upper-case tagName per DOM spec.

html/Element_nodeType.mapsTo: Element.nodeType === 1 (ELEMENT_NODE). Constants exposed on the prototype for React's reconciler hot-path checks.

html/Element_removeAttribute.mapsTo: Element.removeAttribute — also clears data-* dataset entries and re-evaluates overlay heuristic if data-overlay was set.

html/Element_setAttribute.mapsTo: Element.setAttribute(name, value) — handles id, class, data-*, plus presentational width/height for media tags (replay path).

html/Element_textContent.mapsTo: Element.textContent — special-cased for <style>: routes through _processStyleElement instead of setText.

html/Element_value.mapsTo: Element.value — type-aware (range -> normalized, checkbox -> 0/1, progress -> setProgress, default -> setText).

html/Event_constructor.mapsTo: _makeEvent factory in web-compat-element.js. No actual `new Event(...)` constructor surface.

html/StyleSheet_inline.mapsTo: Inline <style> elements parsed and applied to the document. Selectors: tag, .class, #id; combinators NOT supported. Properties routed through CSSStyleDeclaration._applyProperty (full css/* surface).
html/StyleSheet_inline.notes: PR #1345 added :hover; otherwise unchanged from initial implementation. Status flipped to partial (pulp #1434 batch 1) — at-rules and complex selectors are real gaps.

html/button.notes: Inherits ToggleButton widget — toggleable by default.

html/canvas.mapsTo: createCanvas(id, parent). 2D context via Element.getContext('2d'); WebGPU via getContext('webgpu').
html/canvas.notes: See canvas2d/* for the rendering surface.

html/details.mapsTo: createCol(id, parent). The toggle/summary expand-on-click semantics are NOT modeled — children are always rendered.

html/dialog.mapsTo: createPanel(id, parent) + setVisible(false). Modal show()/showModal()/close() not wired.

html/div.notes: Generic container — Yoga column flex by default.

html/document_addEventListener.mapsTo: Global key/mouse listener installation on document.

html/document_createTextNode.notes: Used by React reconciler. Confirmed wired in web-compat-document.js (pulp #1434 batch 1).

html/document_querySelector.mapsTo: document.querySelector — class / id / tag selectors only.

html/h1.notes: Heading levels h1-h6 each map to a Label with default size + weight.

html/img.mapsTo: createLabel(id, '', parent) — placeholder until image loading. attributes are honored via __replayMediaAttributes__ (PR #1347).
unsupportedValues: actual image src loading (placeholder Label is shown)
html/img.notes: Image loading not yet wired through this surface; use <Image> from @pulp/react or the canvas drawImage path instead.

html/input.mapsTo: createTextEditor by default; createFader for type=range, createCheckbox for type=checkbox.
supportedValues: type=text / type=password / type=email / type=number / type=range / type=checkbox

unsupportedValues: the `for` attribute (does not wire focus to a sibling input)
html/label.notes: Confirmed 2026-05-04. Status flipped to partial (pulp #1434 batch 1) — `for` attribute is a real unsupported value, harness flagged DIVERGE.

html/p.notes: Confirmed 2026-05-04. Block-level CSS semantics not modeled (no margin defaults).

html/span.mapsTo:
createLabel(id, '', parent). Confirmed 2026-05-04: maps to Label widget (not View). Same for

,

html/style.mapsTo: Element('style')._ensureNative -> createCol + setVisible(false). textContent is routed through _processStyleElement (web-compat-document.js) which parses CSS rules incl. :hover. PR #1345.
html/style.notes: PR #1345 / pulp #1149 part b. Status flipped to partial (pulp #1434 batch 1) — @media/@keyframes/complex selectors are real gaps.

html/svg.mapsTo: createCol(id, parent) (PR #1347 confirmed). HTML width/height attributes reserve flex layout space; child <path>/<rect>/<circle>/etc are NOT rendered by an SVG renderer — they're parsed as Elements but paint nothing.
supportedValues: <svg> as a layout-leaf placeholder with width/height attrs
unsupportedValues: actual SVG rendering of children (no path/rect/circle/text shape rasterization)
html/svg.notes: PR #1347 (pulp #1147): <svg> is a leaf container that reserves layout space. For rendered SVG paths use the @pulp/react SvgPath intrinsic.

canvas2d._note: Populated 2026-05-04 from web-compat-canvas.js (~36 prototype methods + 7 attrs) crossed against widget_bridge.cpp's canvas* register_function entries (~47 funcs) and the Skia/CG backends in core/canvas/. Spec source: HTML Living Standard CanvasRenderingContext2D / https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D. The bridge layer is consistent across Skia (Linux/Windows) and CG (mac); divergences are noted per-entry. Heavy expansion landed in PR #1348 (pulp #1346 / #1322): save, restore, setTransform, translate, scale, rotate, globalAlpha, globalCompositeOperation, createLinearGradient, createRadialGradient, arc, arcTo, bezierCurveTo, quadraticCurveTo, rect, roundRect, ellipse, clip, fillText, strokeText. Skia gradient shape fills landed in #1353; CG counterpart in #1360 (merged 2026-05-03).

canvas2d/fillRect.mapsTo: web-compat-canvas.js: ctx.fillRect(x,y,w,h) -> _syncGlobalState + _applyFillStyle + canvasRect(id,x,y,w,h). Bridge `canvasRect` honors the active fillStyle / gradient via the use_active_style path (#968).
canvas2d/fillRect.notes: PR #1348 fixed a typo where the JS was calling canvasFillRect (which never existed) — now correctly calls canvasRect.

canvas2d/strokeRect.notes: Stroke gradients fall back to first stop's color (bridge has no stroke-gradient setter today).

canvas2d/rect.mapsTo: ctx.rect(x,y,w,h) -> moveTo + 4× lineTo back to start (composes a closed subpath).
canvas2d/rect.notes: Path-construction op (no fill/stroke). Honors fill()/stroke()/clip() afterward. PR #1348.

canvas2d/roundRect.mapsTo: ctx.roundRect(x,y,w,h,radii) -> moveTo + lineTos + arcTos. Uniform-corner case only.
unsupportedValues: non-uniform radii (4 distinct corners) — falls back to largest single value
canvas2d/roundRect.notes: PR #1348. arcTo approximation is conservative — see canvas2d/arcTo.

canvas2d/arc.mapsTo: ctx.arc(cx,cy,r,start,end,anticlockwise) -> path-mode emit as cubic-bezier segments via canvasMoveTo + canvasCubicTo. Bridge has immediate-mode canvasArc but no path-mode arc primitive.
canvas2d/arc.notes: PR #1348. The bezier approximation is per-quadrant (k = (4/3)·tan(θ/4)); fidelity is visually exact for fill/stroke/clip use cases.

canvas2d/arcTo.mapsTo: ctx.arcTo(x1,y1,x2,y2,r) -> conservative lineTo(x1,y1) + lineTo(x2,y2). Radius is currently ignored.
unsupportedValues: geometrically-correct arcTo with the proper radius-tangent computation
canvas2d/arcTo.notes: PR #1348. Used by roundRect and FilterBank's marquee corners; fidelity loss is minimal for those use cases. File a follow-up if a plugin needs spec-compliant arcTo.

canvas2d/ellipse.mapsTo: ctx.ellipse(cx,cy,rx,ry,rotation,start,end,anticlockwise) -> rotation IGNORED; if rx===ry falls through to arc; otherwise emits a 4-segment cubic approximation.
canvas2d/ellipse.notes: PR #1348. FilterBank doesn't use ellipse — this is a thin parity shim.

unsupportedValues: fill rule arg ('evenodd' / 'nonzero') — accepted but ignored

canvas2d/clip.mapsTo: ctx.clip(fillRule) -> canvasClip (intersects current clip with current path). Falls back to no-op if canvasClip absent.
unsupportedValues: fillRule arg — bridge always uses non-zero today
canvas2d/clip.notes: Backed by SkCanvas::clipPath. (issue-896 added canvasClip; older canvasClipRect is the rect-only path.)

canvas2d/save.notes: PR #1348. Without save/restore, FilterBank's first ctx.save() threw TypeError and aborted the frame.

canvas2d/setTransform.mapsTo: ctx.setTransform(a,b,c,d,e,f) — also accepts a single DOMMatrix-like arg, shim unpacks. -> canvasSetTransform.

canvas2d/transform.mapsTo: ctx.transform(a,b,c,d,e,f) — multiplies CURRENT transform; bridge only exposes replace (setTransform), not concat. Pure-translation matrices (a===1, b===0, c===0, d===1) fall through to canvasTranslate; everything else is a no-op.
canvas2d/transform.notes: PR #1348. File a follow-up if a plugin needs strict concat.

canvas2d/fillText.mapsTo: ctx.fillText(text,x,y,maxWidth) -> _syncGlobalState + _syncTextState + _applyFillStyle + canvasFillText(id, text, x, y, size, color). maxWidth is ignored; gradient fillStyle uses the first stop's color.
canvas2d/fillText.notes: PR #1348. canvasSetFont parses 'Npx Family' from the JS-side font string.

canvas2d/strokeText.mapsTo: ctx.strokeText -> falls back to fillText with the strokeStyle color (bridge has no stroke-text command).
supportedValues: visual approximation of stroked text via solid fill
canvas2d/strokeText.notes: Visually close enough for FilterBank/HUD use; file a follow-up if a plugin needs outlined typography.

canvas2d/measureText.mapsTo: ctx.measureText(text) — when canvasMeasureText is available returns the bridge-side TextMetrics; else returns a coarse char-width estimate.
canvas2d/measureText.notes: issue-916. The shim guarantees a non-null TextMetrics object even on older hosts where canvasMeasureText isn't registered (returns zero-filled fields based on font px size).

canvas2d/drawImage.mapsTo: ctx.drawImage(img, ...) -> canvasDrawImage(id, src, dx, dy, dw, dh). Supports the 3-arg, 5-arg, and 9-arg signatures; the 9-arg source-rect form is currently treated as the destination-only form (sx/sy/sw/sh ignored).
unsupportedValues: drawImage(img, sx, sy, sw, sh, dx, dy, dw, dh) — sprite-sheet slicing not yet wired
canvas2d/drawImage.notes: issue-916. File a follow-up if a Pulp plugin needs sprite-sheet slicing.

canvas2d/getImageData.mapsTo: ctx.getImageData(x,y,w,h) -> canvasGetImageData (returns base64 RGBA blob, shim decodes to Uint8ClampedArray).
supportedValues: any rect from a rasterized canvas
canvas2d/getImageData.notes: issue-916. The base64 round-trip avoids passing typed arrays across the JS bridge.

canvas2d/putImageData.mapsTo: ctx.putImageData(imgdata, dx, dy) -> base64-encodes and calls canvasPutImageData.
unsupportedValues: putImageData(img, dx, dy, dirtyX, dirtyY, dirtyW, dirtyH) — sub-rect form treated as no-rect
canvas2d/putImageData.notes: issue-916. File a follow-up if sub-rect updates become a hot path.

canvas2d/createLinearGradient.mapsTo: ctx.createLinearGradient(x0,y0,x1,y1) -> returns a JS-side CanvasGradient object with addColorStop(); the bridge picks it up on the next fill via canvasSetLinearGradient.
canvas2d/createLinearGradient.notes: PR #1348. CG (mac) gradient-shape fill landed in #1359/#1360 (merged 2026-05-03).

canvas2d/createRadialGradient.mapsTo: ctx.createRadialGradient(x0,y0,r0,x1,y1,r1) -> CanvasGradient with kind='radial'. Bridge models a single-circle radial gradient (centre + radius); shim uses the OUTER circle (x1,y1,r1).
supportedValues: centre-bloom usage where x0===x1, y0===y1, r0===0
unsupportedValues: true two-circle radial gradient (inner + outer with separate radii)
canvas2d/createRadialGradient.notes: PR #1348. Visually equivalent for FilterBank's typical centre-bloom; file follow-up if a plugin needs the offset-inner-circle form.

canvas2d/createConicGradient.mapsTo: ctx.createConicGradient -> shim returns an empty linear gradient as a graceful fallback (consumer code can assign to fillStyle without throwing). Bridge has no conic shader.
canvas2d/createConicGradient.notes: Conic gradients need a Skia conic shader — not yet plumbed through. Silent degradation to linear (likely invisible). File a follow-up before any plugin actually depends on conic.

canvas2d/createPattern.mapsTo: ctx.createPattern -> shim returns null (per spec, returning null is permissible when source unavailable). Bridge has no pattern shader.
canvas2d/createPattern.notes: Patterns are rare in audio plugin UIs. Callers must guard against null.

canvas2d/addColorStop.mapsTo: CanvasGradient.addColorStop(offset, color) — accumulates stops on the JS-side gradient object; flushed to bridge via canvasSetLinearGradient / canvasSetRadialGradient when the gradient is assigned to fillStyle.

unsupportedValues: odd-length array spec-doubling (shim takes pattern verbatim; bridge handles the spec-doubling)

canvas2d/getLineDash.mapsTo: Returns a copy of the current pattern (per spec).

canvas2d/lineDashOffset.mapsTo: Tracked as a JS field; passed to canvasSetLineDash on every setLineDash call. Mutating lineDashOffset alone (without re-calling setLineDash) does NOT push the new offset to the bridge.
unsupportedValues: lineDashOffset mutation between draws (must re-call setLineDash to push new phase)
canvas2d/lineDashOffset.notes: Minor edge case; file a follow-up if observable.

canvas2d/fillStyle.mapsTo: Settable to a CSS-color string OR a CanvasGradient object. Solid colors push via canvasSetFillColor; gradients push via canvasSetLinearGradient / canvasSetRadialGradient on the next draw.

canvas2d/strokeStyle.mapsTo: Settable to a CSS-color string OR a CanvasGradient. Bridge has no stroke-gradient setter — gradient strokeStyle degrades to the first stop's color.
unsupportedValues: CanvasGradient on stroke (degrades to first-stop color)
canvas2d/strokeStyle.notes: Bridge could expose canvasSetStrokeLinearGradient if a plugin needs gradient strokes.

canvas2d/lineWidth.mapsTo: Tracked locally; pushed to bridge via canvasSetLineWidth on the next stroke().

canvas2d/lineCap.mapsTo: Locally tracked; flushed via canvasSetLineCap on next stroke (idempotent: only pushes when value changed).

canvas2d/lineJoin.mapsTo: Locally tracked; flushed via canvasSetLineJoin on next stroke.

canvas2d/miterLimit.mapsTo: Tracked locally; not pushed to bridge.
canvas2d/miterLimit.notes: Silent no-op — bridge has no canvasSetMiterLimit. File a follow-up if needed.

canvas2d/globalAlpha.mapsTo: Locally tracked; flushed via canvasSetGlobalAlpha on the next draw.

canvas2d/globalCompositeOperation.mapsTo: Locally tracked; flushed via canvasGlobalCompositeOperation OR canvasSetBlendMode on the next draw.
canvas2d/globalCompositeOperation.notes: PR #1348. Both backends now honor every value: Skia handles them via SkBlendMode; CG via CGContextSetBlendMode (pulp #1371 fixed a silent no-op in CoreGraphicsCanvas::set_blend_mode and added pixel-level coverage in test_canvas.cpp).

canvas2d/font.mapsTo: Locally tracked; canvasSetFont(id, family, size) on the next draw. Shim parses '<size>px <family>'.
unsupportedValues: full CSS font shorthand (style/variant/stretch — only size+family extracted)

canvas2d/textAlign.mapsTo: Locally tracked; flushed via canvasSetTextAlign on next text draw.

canvas2d/imageSmoothingEnabled.mapsTo: Tracked as a JS field; not pushed to bridge.
canvas2d/imageSmoothingEnabled.notes: Silent no-op. Skia/CG default to enabled smoothing.

canvas2d/imageSmoothingQuality.mapsTo: Tracked as a JS field; not pushed to bridge.

canvas2d/direction.mapsTo: Tracked locally; not pushed to bridge.
canvas2d/direction.notes: Silent no-op. Yoga RTL is also unsupported (yoga/direction missing).

canvas2d/filter.mapsTo: Not implemented in shim or bridge.
canvas2d/filter.notes: Distinct from CSS `filter` on the View — this is the per-context Canvas2D filter. Not yet implemented.

canvas2d/shadowColor.mapsTo: ctx.shadowColor -> canvasSetShadowColor (web-compat-canvas.js _syncShadowState). Sticky state on the C++ canvas; backends apply via SkImageFilters::DropShadow (Skia) or CGContextSetShadowWithColor (CoreGraphics).
supportedValues: CSS color string (#rgb, #rrggbb, rgba(...), hsl(...), named colors, transparent)
canvas2d/shadowColor.notes: issue-1434 batch 7. Box-shadow on the View widget remains the separate setBoxShadow primitive (#925).

canvas2d/shadowBlur.mapsTo: ctx.shadowBlur -> canvasSetShadowBlur (web-compat-canvas.js _syncShadowState). Skia maps blur radius to Gaussian sigma via sigma = blur/2.0 (Chromium's mapping); CG passes blur radius through directly.
canvas2d/shadowBlur.notes: issue-1434 batch 7. HTML5 spec: negative values are silently ignored at the shim/bridge boundary.

canvas2d/shadowOffsetX.mapsTo: ctx.shadowOffsetX -> canvasSetShadowOffsetX (web-compat-canvas.js _syncShadowState). Combines with shadowOffsetY into the dx/dy components of the Skia DropShadow filter / CG shadow offset CGSize.
canvas2d/shadowOffsetX.notes: issue-1434 batch 7. Non-finite values are silently ignored by the shim.

canvas2d/shadowOffsetY.mapsTo: ctx.shadowOffsetY -> canvasSetShadowOffsetY (web-compat-canvas.js _syncShadowState). Combines with shadowOffsetX; same Skia / CG mapping.
canvas2d/shadowOffsetY.notes: issue-1434 batch 7. Non-finite values are silently ignored by the shim.

canvas2d/getContext_webgpu.mapsTo: HTMLCanvasElement.getContext('webgpu') -> __createGPUCanvasContext (web-compat-canvas.js). Returns a GPUCanvasContext with configure / getCurrentTexture / present that bridges to native WebGPU when the device is native.
canvas2d/getContext_webgpu.notes: Tracked in detail under canvas2d/* by convention even though WebGPU is not strictly Canvas2D — the shim co-locates them. See planning/research/three-js* for the Three.js end-to-end story.

canvas2d/_native_canvasFillCircle.mapsTo: Pulp-specific bridge fn (canvasFillCircle). Used by some legacy widget renderers; NOT spec'd in HTML5 Canvas. Plugin code should prefer arc()+fill().
canvas2d/_native_canvasFillCircle.notes: Bridge convenience; not part of the standard Canvas2D API.

canvas2d/_native_canvasFillRoundedRect.mapsTo: Pulp-specific bridge fn (canvasFillRoundedRect). Equivalent of roundRect()+fill() in one call.

canvas2d/_native_canvasStrokeLine.mapsTo: Pulp-specific bridge fn (canvasStrokeLine). Equivalent of moveTo()+lineTo()+stroke() in one call.

imports._note: The 'imports' prefix tracks design-import format detection (see core/dsl/import-design / tools/cli/src/import_design.cpp / docs/reference/imports/*). Each top-level key is a recognized import source (claude, figma, pencil, stitch, v0); each entry carries 'parser-version' (the Pulp-side detector version) and 'detected-formats' (a list of {format-version, introduced, deprecated, fingerprint, notes} objects). The `fingerprint` field is the heuristic the parser uses to recognize the source format from a directory or file payload. When a new import source lands or an existing source's export format changes, add a detected-formats entry rather than mutating an existing one (so historical exports keep parsing). Tracked via PR #1047 (pulp #1031) for versioned import-design detection.

detected-formats.notes: Anthropic Labs Claude Design — manually-exported standalone HTML. Detect via the inline bundler script tag inserted by the Claude Design export. Loader-shell HTML wraps the bundled React app; the static parser sees ~9 elements until the bundle is executed.

Material 3 token vocab. Stitch ZIP / directory exports include code.html, DESIGN.md, screen.png at the top level. Tailwind CDN configured with the forms + container-queries plugins is the strongest single signal.
